bugs.error_message: phase-19-owui-nightly: couldn't find env file /home/runner/work/hive/hive/.env.ci (exit 1, 7 consecutive nightly fails since 2026-06-26)
bugs.root_cause: workflow referenced ../../.env.ci but no step created it; file never in tree, never gitignored, no other workflow uses it
bugs.fix: PR #262: Materialize .env.ci step before compose boot, built from GH secrets (mirrors live-integration secret set), chmod 600, hard-fail loop on empty required externals, OWUI_SHIM_KEY generated per run

bugs.error_message: OWUI Playwright nightly: all 14 specs ENOENT e2e/phase-19/owui/.auth/owui-user.json; suite never green in history
bugs.root_cause: OWUI_E2E_EMAIL/OWUI_E2E_PASSWORD repo secrets never provisioned; owui.setup.ts setup.skip() on missing creds does not stop dependent projects, storageState file never written
bugs.fix: PR #266: playwright.owui.config.ts gates owui/owui-perf projects with testMatch [] when creds absent (clean skip, exit 0) + workflow ::warning:: annotation. Real coverage still requires provisioning the two secrets

bugs.error_message: OWUI nightly setup: 'Sign in with Hive' OIDC button never renders; locator.click timeout 30s at owui.setup.ts:14 (run 28646650000)
bugs.root_cause: OWUI OAUTH_CLIENT_ID/SECRET map to SUPABASE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID/SECRET which default empty; nightly .env.ci materialization never writes them and no repo secret exists; empty client id means OWUI never registers OIDC provider, only native login card renders
bugs.fix: Issue #269 filed. Needs owner: add SUPABASE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID/SECRET repo secrets (values from GoTrue OAuth app registered for OWUI in SSO #259 setup), then wire into .env.ci block + readiness assert

bugs.error_message: TS2307 cannot find module 'recharts' in web-console CI (PR #314)
bugs.root_cause: Dead-code grep scoped to apps/web-console/src only; recharts imports live in apps/web-console/components/analytics/{error,spend,usage}-chart.tsx, so the dep was wrongly judged unused and removed
bugs.fix: Reverted package.json + package-lock.json to origin/main byte-identical; rule: dependency-usage grep must cover the whole package dir (components/, app/, tests/), never just src/

bugs.error_message: gh pr edit / gh issue view fail: classic Projects GraphQL field deprecated (projectCards)
bugs.root_cause: Repo linked to classic Projects; default gh commands query deprecated projectCards GraphQL field
bugs.fix: Use gh issue view --json <fields> and gh api PATCH /repos/.../pulls/N instead of gh pr edit

bugs.error_message: git pull --ff-only aborts: untracked working tree files would be overwritten by merge (.wolf/, .planning/carl/, spec)
bugs.root_cause: PR #314 committed snapshots of files that existed locally as untracked; pull refuses to overwrite untracked paths
bugs.fix: Verify backup (diff -rq) then move collision paths aside, pull, restore live .wolf copy over tracked snapshot (drift shows as M, committed next .wolf pass)

bugs.error_message: gh pr merge failed: Pull Request has merge conflicts (mergePullRequest); second PR editing tools/lint-no-direct-tenant-id.mjs allowlist conflicted after first PR merged
bugs.root_cause: Parallel builder PRs (#324, #326) both appended entries to the same ALLOWLIST_DIRS block in the tenant-id lint script; first squash merge made the second PR conflict.
bugs.fix: Fresh worktree from remote branch, git merge origin/main, union both allowlist entries with their justification comments, push, re-gate, merge. Prevention: treat lint allowlist files as a known shared-file hotspot for parallel PR waves; assign allowlist edits line-disjoint or merge serially.

bugs.error_message: cannot delete branch used by worktree / premature local cleanup while PR still OPEN after failed merge
bugs.root_cause: Orchestrator compound command ran worktree remove + branch -D in the same command as gh pr merge, so cleanup executed even when the merge failed (conflict), destroying the builder local state early.
bugs.fix: Sequence strictly: merge, verify state==MERGED via gh pr view, only then remove worktree and delete local branch. Remote branch preserved recovery this time.

bugs.error_message: Cross-tenant data leak via permissive service-scan RLS policy USING(true) TO hive_app on agent_tasks (PR #333, caught in review pre-merge)
bugs.root_cause: Postgres OR-combines all PERMISSIVE policies for a role+command. A blanket USING(true) SELECT policy added for a background worker cancels the tenant_isolation policy for EVERY hive_app SELECT, because Get/List filter tenant via RLS only (not in SQL WHERE). Third occurrence of this class (egress #308, RAG #232 prior).
bugs.fix: Never add a blanket table RLS policy to the shared app role for a cross-tenant background job. Use a SECURITY DEFINER function scoped to exactly the job query, or a dedicated BYPASSRLS role/connection. Always add a regression test: a 2-tenant authenticated user still cannot Get/List another tenant row after the migration.

bugs.error_message: Web E2E (full stack) CI: container hive-control-plane-1 is unhealthy; air build fails: no required module provides package .../apps/agent-engine/engineapi; container exit 127 loop
bugs.root_cause: PR #332 added agent-engine import to control-plane main.go (local go.mod replace), but deploy/docker/Dockerfile.control-plane copied only agent-engine go.mod/go.sum for cache warmup, never the source tree. Control-plane builds at container boot via air, so gap surfaced only at runtime in E2E, not in build-time images or go-tests.
bugs.fix: Add COPY apps/agent-engine/ ./apps/agent-engine/ to Dockerfile.control-plane, matching existing pattern for control-plane/storage/audit-canonical local modules. PR #340.

bugs.error_message: column "tenant_id" does not exist on clean supabase db push at 20260625_06_audit_cold_archive_manifest.sql (CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ... (tenant_id, partition_month))
bugs.root_cause: audit_cold_archive_manifest defined twice with incompatible shapes: 20260516_04 (May, partition_name/parquet_path) and 20260625_06 (June, id/tenant_id/object_key). June used CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, so on a chained fresh apply it no-opped onto the May table, then its unique index on the June-only column tenant_id errored. A CI-only DROP TABLE band-aid in ci.yml masked it, and a stale compliance test (audit_retention_test.go) asserted May columns, so it only passed because the CI drop left the May table live. App code (auditarchive/pg_repository.go) and the 20260625_08 immutability trigger both use the June shape, so June is authoritative.
bugs.fix: Made 20260625_06 self-healing (permitted: it was un-applied and always-failing): drop the stale May table and its May-era policies (manifest_service_role_all, manifest_auditor_select) then create the June table, with hive_app INSERT/SELECT/UPDATE and auditor_ro SELECT grants relocated in. Removed the CI DROP band-aid so CI validates the real chain. Fixed audit_retention_test.go to assert June columns. Stale May table provably empty everywhere (app writes June-only columns, so writes against the May shape always error; staging confirmed 0 rows), so the drop preserves all audit data. Validated: full migration chain applies cleanly on a fresh pgvector:pg17 with no drop hack, and the compliance + RLS suites pass against the June schema.

bugs.error_message: Desktop sandbox launch() could hang the whole app (once a 15+ minute cargo test hang) inside the Command::pre_exec closure
bugs.root_cause: The pre_exec closure ran after fork() and before execve() in a child of a multithreaded process but was not async-signal-safe: it built the Landlock ruleset (Ruleset build, PathFd::new), compiled the seccomp BPF program, ran codex pre_main_hardening (std::env::remove_var over vars_os), and used format! on error paths. Allocating in a forked child can deadlock forever if another thread held the allocator lock at fork, and std makes the parent spawn() block until the child execs or errors, so the parent hangs too.
bugs.fix: Hoist all allocation and fd setup before the fork: build_landlock_ruleset returns a RulesetCreated (converted to OwnedFd) and build_seccomp_filter returns the compiled BpfProgram, both in the parent. Strip LD_* env on the Command before fork instead of via std::env in the child. The closure now performs only raw async-signal-safe syscalls on pre-built values: prctl(PR_SET_DUMPABLE) via codex disable_process_dumping, setrlimit(RLIMIT_CORE), prctl(PR_SET_NO_NEW_PRIVS), raw landlock_restrict_self on the pre-built fd, and seccompiler::apply_filter over the pre-compiled program; error paths map to allocation-free io::Errors. Added tests/launch_spawn.rs end-to-end regression guard. The #341 10s spawn timeout stays as defense in depth.

bugs.error_message: duplicate key value violates unique constraint "idx_request_attempts_account_request_attempt" (SQLSTATE 23505) on chat dispatch
bugs.root_cause: Non-idempotent request-attempt insert. A single edge-api chat request starts the same attempt twice for one (account_id, request_id, attempt_number=1): the orchestrator calls StartAttempt directly (status dispatching), then CreateReservation internally calls usage.StartAttempt again (accounting/service.go). usage/repository.go CreateAttempt used a plain INSERT, so the second insert hit the unique index and raised 23505. Non-fatal to the served request (errors were logged as non-fatal), but the error aborted the reservation transaction, silently dropping the credit hold, and polluted logs.
bugs.fix: Make CreateAttempt idempotent with INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (account_id, request_id, attempt_number) DO UPDATE SET status = request_attempts.status RETURNING ... The no-op SET returns the pre-existing attempt (first write wins); status transitions stay owned by UpdateAttemptStatus so an attempt never moves backward. Preserves append-only semantics and money-path correctness (single attempt, single reservation, no double-charge). Added DB-backed regression test TestCreateAttemptIsIdempotentOnAccountRequestAttempt in apps/control-plane/internal/usage/repository_test.go asserting no 23505, same row returned, exactly one row.

bugs.error_message: agent-subsystem /v1 endpoints (/v1/rag/*, /v1/agent/tasks*, /v1/artifacts*) 404 via UnsupportedEndpointMiddleware; entire agent-subsystem web surface dead (demo blocker)
bugs.root_cause: support matrix supported_now never updated for Waves 2-4 endpoints. The edge-api UnsupportedEndpointMiddleware 404s any /v1/ path not marked supported_now in packages/openai-contract/matrix/support-matrix.json (loaded at boot, baked into the Docker image). The rag/agent/artifacts handlers shipped wired and feature-gated but were only covered by per-package isolation tests that bypass the full middleware chain, so CI stayed green while every request 404-ed in a real stack. Note: support-matrix.json is the hand-maintained source; overlays/hive-support-status.yaml is an OpenAI-only OpenAPI overlay consumed by nothing and does not feed the matrix.
bugs.fix: Add supported_now entries for the 13 real proprietary routes (verified against the router registrations, not the ticket's guide) to support-matrix.json, then regenerate docs/support-matrix.md + generated/hive-openapi.yaml via generate-matrix.sh. Add apps/edge-api/internal/middleware/unsupported_integration_test.go, a full-chain integration test that drives all 13 paths through the real UnsupportedEndpointMiddleware + the real committed matrix: 404 before, 200 after.
